##NAMES=Hagstromite
##RRUFFID=R250066
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Pb_8_Cu^2+^(Te^6+^O_6_)_2_(CO_3_)Cl_4_
##LOCALITY=Otto Mountain, Baker, California, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Light yellow–green  transparent blades on quartz in association with cerussite, fuettererite and thorneite. Type sample, described in Kampf et al (2020)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250066
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=Pb_8.07_Cu^2+^_0.98_Te^6+^_1.96_C_1.17_Cl_3.83_O_15.34_ ; The small fragile crystals embedded in epoxy could not be polished as they fragmented even when subjected to gentle abrasion. Analyses were eventually done on unpolished crystal surfaces.
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 23.699 b: 9.0487 c: 10.4693 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 2245.1 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
